---
title: "Test Merge House Price Data"
author: "Jacopo Olivieri"
date: "2024-01-13"
output:
  html_document:
    fig_width: 6
    fig_aspect_ratio: 0.618
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    echo: false
    warning: false
    message: false
    fig.align: "center"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Initialize packages with version control
if (!requireNamespace("renv", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("renv")
}

# Run manual renv commands if needed
# renv::activate() # Activate the project library
# renv::restore()  # Restore the environment if running for first time
# renv::snapshot()  # After adding/updating packages, snapshot the state

# Load required packages
required_packages <- c(
  "rmarkdown", "rio", "tidyverse", "purrr", "here",
  "janitor", "data.table", "lubridate", "rnrfa", "sf",
  "PostcodesioR", "furrr", "future", "memoise"
)
install_if_missing <- function(packages) {
  new_packages <- packages[!sapply(packages, requireNamespace, quietly = TRUE)]
  if (length(new_packages) > 0) {
    install.packages(new_packages)
  }
  invisible(sapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE))
}
install_if_missing(required_packages)

# set up parallel processing
setup_parallel <- function() {
  n_workers <- parallelly::availableCores() - 1
  machine_ram <- memuse::Sys.meminfo()$totalram
  ram_limit <- as.numeric(0.7 * machine_ram)

  options(future.globals.maxSize = ram_limit)
  future::plan(future::multisession, workers = n_workers)
  logger::log_info("Parallel processing initialized with {n_workers} workers")
}
setup_parallel()


# Initialize logging
log_path <- here::here("output", "log", "test_merge_house_price_data.log")
if (!dir.exists(dirname(log_path))) {
  dir.create(dirname(log_path), recursive = TRUE)
}
logger::log_appender(logger::appender_file(log_path))
logger::log_layout(logger::layout_glue_colors)
```

## Load EDM Data

```{r, load_data}
dat <- import(here(
  "data", "processed",
  "individual_edm_location.RDATA"
), trust = TRUE)

dat_yr <- dat$yearly
dat_mo <- dat$monthly
```

## Load House Price Data


```{r}
house_price_data <- import(here("data", "processed", "house_price.rds"),
  trust = TRUE
)

house_price_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(latitude))
```


## Test Process Postcodes Function

### Plug and play


```{r}
x <- raw_data %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  slice_head(n = 100) %>%
  ungroup()

x <- raw_data %>%
  slice(1201:1300)

process_postcodes <- function(postcodes, batch_size = 100) {
  n_batches <- ceiling(length(postcodes) / batch_size)

  # Split postcodes into batches
  batch_indices <- split(
    seq_along(postcodes),
    ceiling(seq_along(postcodes) / batch_size)
  )

  # Process batches in parallel
  logger::log_info("Processing {length(postcodes)} postcodes in {n_batches} batches")

  results <- future_map(batch_indices, function(idx) {
    batch_postcodes <- list(postcodes = postcodes[idx])
    batch_result <- bulk_postcode_lookup(batch_postcodes)

    map(batch_result, function(x) {
      res <- x$result
      res <- res[names(res) %in% CONFIG$postcode_vars]
      res
    })
  }, .progress = TRUE)

  # Combine all results using data.table
  logger::log_info("Combining results into single data frame")
  data.table::rbindlist(
    unlist(results, recursive = FALSE),
    fill = TRUE,
    use.names = TRUE
  ) %>%
    as_tibble()
}



y <- process_postcodes(raw_data$postcode)
y

z <- bind_rows(y)
bind_rows(head(y))

head(y, 1)
```

### Test batch binding

```{r}
library(microbenchmark)
library(dplyr)
library(data.table)

test_data <- y[[1]]
test_dt()
test_dplyr()
test_base()

test_dt <- function() {
  data.table::rbindlist(test_data, fill = TRUE, use.names = TRUE)
}
test_dplyr <- function() bind_rows(test_data)
test_base <- function() {
  # Convert NULLs to NA before converting to data.frame
  processed_data <- lapply(test_data, function(x) {
    x$admin_county <- if (is.null(x$admin_county)) NA else x$admin_county
    as.data.frame(x, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  })
  do.call(rbind, processed_data)
}

# Run benchmark
results <- microbenchmark(
  data.table = test_dt(),
  dplyr = test_dplyr(),
  base = test_base(),
  times = 50
)

# Print results
print(results)
```

```{r}
test_data <- y[1:100]

test_nested <- function() {
  data.table::rbindlist(
    lapply(test_data, function(x) data.table::rbindlist(x, fill = TRUE, use.names = FALSE)),
    fill = TRUE,
    use.names = TRUE
  )
}
test_flat <- function() {
  data.table::rbindlist(
    unlist(test_data, recursive = FALSE),
    fill = TRUE,
    use.names = TRUE
  )
}
test_flat2 <- function() {
  # Then use rbindlist on the flattened result
  data.table::rbindlist(
    unlist(test_data, recursive = FALSE),
    fill = TRUE,
    use.names = TRUE
  )
}
test_dplyr <- function() bind_rows(test_data)


results <- microbenchmark(
  flat2 = test_flat2(),
  nested = test_nested(),
  flat = test_flat(),
  dplyr = test_dplyr(),
  times = 100
) %>%
  suppressWarnings()

print(results)

# Verify they give same result
result_nested <- test_nested()
result_flat <- test_flat()
result_flat2 <- test_flat2()
result_dplyr <- test_dplyr()
identical(result_nested, result_flat)
identical(result_flat, result_flat2)
identical(result_flat, as.data.table(result_dplyr))
# I WOULD DO FLAT2
```

### Test future map vs map

```{r}
library(microbenchmark)
setup_parallel <- function() {
  n_workers <- parallelly::availableCores() # - 1
  machine_ram <- memuse::Sys.meminfo()$totalram
  ram_limit <- as.numeric(0.9 * machine_ram)

  options(future.globals.maxSize = ram_limit)
  future::plan(future::multisession, workers = n_workers)
  logger::log_info("Parallel processing initialized with {n_workers} workers")
}
setup_parallel()


x <- raw_data %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  slice_head(n = 3000) %>%
  ungroup()

process_postcodes_future <- function(postcodes, batch_size = 100) {
  n_batches <- ceiling(length(postcodes) / batch_size)

  # Split postcodes into batches
  batch_indices <- split(
    seq_along(postcodes),
    ceiling(seq_along(postcodes) / batch_size)
  )

  # Process batches in parallel
  logger::log_info("Processing {length(postcodes)} postcodes in {n_batches} batches")

  results <- future_map(batch_indices, function(idx) {
    batch_postcodes <- list(postcodes = postcodes[idx])
    batch_result <- bulk_postcode_lookup(batch_postcodes)

    map(batch_result, function(x) {
      res <- x$result
      res <- res[names(res) %in% CONFIG$postcode_vars]
      res
    })
  }, .progress = TRUE)

  # Combine all results using data.table
  logger::log_info("Combining results into single data frame")
  data.table::rbindlist(
    unlist(results, recursive = FALSE),
    fill = TRUE,
    use.names = TRUE
  ) %>%
    as_tibble() %>%
    suppressWarnings()
}

process_postcodes_sequential <- function(postcodes, batch_size = 100) {
  n_batches <- ceiling(length(postcodes) / batch_size)

  batch_indices <- split(
    seq_along(postcodes),
    ceiling(seq_along(postcodes) / batch_size)
  )

  logger::log_info("Processing {length(postcodes)} postcodes in {n_batches} batches")

  results <- map(batch_indices, function(idx) {
    batch_postcodes <- list(postcodes = postcodes[idx])
    batch_result <- bulk_postcode_lookup(batch_postcodes)

    map(batch_result, function(x) {
      res <- x$result
      res <- res[names(res) %in% CONFIG$postcode_vars]
      res
    })
  }, .progress = TRUE)

  logger::log_info("Combining results into single data frame")
  data.table::rbindlist(
    unlist(results, recursive = FALSE),
    fill = TRUE,
    use.names = TRUE
  ) %>%
    as_tibble() %>%
    suppressWarnings()
}

benchmark_results <- microbenchmark(
  batch_50 = process_postcodes_future(x$postcode, batch_size = 50),
  batch_100 = process_postcodes_future(x$postcode, batch_size = 100),
  sequential50 = process_postcodes_sequential(x$postcode, batch_size = 50),
  sequential100 = process_postcodes_sequential(x$postcode, batch_size = 100),
  times = 10
)


print(benchmark_results)

autoplot(benchmark_results) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Postcode Processing: Parallel vs Sequential",
    subtitle = paste(
      "Based on", length(x$postcode), "postcodes,",
      parallelly::availableCores(), "workers"
    ),
    y = "Time (milliseconds)"
  )
```

```{r}
postcode_data <- postcode_data %>%
  mutate(postcode = str_remove_all(postcode, fixed(" "))) %>%
  unique()

left_join(raw_data, postcode_data, by = join_by(postcode))
```
